items.title: Welcome reception
items.note: Optional · Drinks on your own

items.title: Registration
items.location: Wolfe's Neck Center for Agriculture & the Environment · Freeport

items.title: Reconnection, gather with friends new and old, Guild introduction circles
Welcome, Zander Evans, & keynote: "A Perspective on the Future of Forest Stewardship", Laura McCarthy, New Mexico State Forester
Working Session 1: Indigenous stewardship & ecological forestry
items.note: An open conversation on concrete examples of how Indigenous stewardship and traditional ecological knowledge have been incorporated into ecological forestry on non-tribal lands.

items.title: Working Session 1: Stewardship for Land Conservation
items.note: How land trusts are stewarding conservation lands, plus regional challenges and approaches. Facilitator: Erika Rowland.

items.title: Working Session 1: Forest Health Update
items.note: Flash presentations on HWA, buckthorn, Beech Leaf Disease, Eastern Spruce Budworm, and Mountain Pine Beetle, followed by discussion.

items.title: Lunch
items.note: Catered lunch, included in registration

items.title: Working Session 2: Science and Policy for Ecological Integrity
items.note: Defining and measuring ecological integrity, drawing on FSC standards and the Guild's biomass harvesting guidelines. Facilitator: SJ Brown.

items.title: Working Session 2: Engaging Landowners in Stewardship
items.note: Lessons from Women Owning Woodlands, Fire Adapted Communities, and Forestry for the Birds. Facilitators: Mike Lynch & Sarah Hagan.

items.title: Working Session 2: Sustaining Ash Partnership Network
items.note: Learn about SAP-Ne and work toward its extension and adoption. Facilitator: Rachel Swanwick.

items.title: Working Session 3: Markets to Support Ecological Forestry
items.note: Timber, carbon, water, and recreation markets that pay for work in the woods. Facilitator: Mike Lynch.

items.title: Working Session 3: Welcoming All Stewards
items.note: Best practices for welcoming and supporting all stewardship and expanding membership. Facilitator: Sarah Hagan.

items.title: Working Session 3: Beneficial Fire
items.note: Cultural burning, prescribed fire, and managed wildfire as tools for resilient ecosystems. Facilitator: Esme Cadiente.

items.title: Reception dinner
items.note: Included in registration (end time estimated, around 8:30 PM)

items.title: Half-day field trip, choice of three (New England Forestry Foundation Bliss Woods Preserve / Brunswick-Topsham Land Trust Crystal Spring Farm / Wolfe's Neck Woods State Park)
items.note: Lunch included. You do not choose your field trip at registration, the team will follow up separately to arrange choices.

items.title: Lunch
items.note: Return from field trips for lunch

items.title: Working Session 4: Stewarding Old Forests in a Changing Climate
items.note: Advancing tools, techniques, and policy for old forests, building on the Guild's Position Statement on Stewardship of Old Forests and Trees. Facilitator: Kristen Pelz.

items.title: Working Session 4: Workforce Development
items.note: Strategies for engaging students, creating apprenticeships, and supporting early career stewards. Facilitator: Matt Piccarello.

items.title: Working Session 4: Extreme Weather
items.note: Mitigation and response strategies for floods, ice storms, and hurricanes, including forest-type specific approaches.

items.title: Working session action items: Advancing stewardship and ecological forestry
items.note: Opportunities for action at the individual member and Guild organizational level.

items.title: Working session action items: Outreach and engagement actions
items.note: Opportunities for action at the individual member and Guild organizational level.

items.title: Working session action items: Action items by region
items.note: Opportunities for action at the individual member and Guild organizational level.

items.title: Catered dinner
items.note: Included in registration (end time estimated, around 8:30 PM)

items.title: Holt Research Forest (pending), The Nature Conservancy's Mill Pond Preserve (pending)
items.note: Bag lunch included (exact window TBD, estimated 8:00 AM–4:00 PM for calendar purposes)

items.title: Ash treatments demo, Maine Audubon's Gilsland Farm; Massabesic Experimental Forest, US Forest Service
items.location: Falmouth & Alfred/Lyman, ME · Under 40 min from venue
items.note: Bag lunch included · Transportation provided (exact window TBD, estimated 8:00 AM–4:00 PM for calendar purposes)
